title: becwright rules
description: Schema for .bec/rules.yaml — the rules becwright enforces on every commit. Field reference: https://github.com/DataDave-Dev/becwright/blob/main/documentation/usage.md

schema_version.description: Format version of this file; absent means 1. becwright refuses a file stamped newer than it understands.

global_exclude.description: Global glob patterns of files to exclude from all file-based rules.

rules.description: The rules (BECs) enforced on every commit.

intent.description: What the rule asks for — the 'bound' half of the BEC.

why_it_matters.description: Why the rule exists; shown the moment the rule fails.

rejected_alternatives.description: Context: approaches that were considered and dropped.

paths.description: Glob patterns of the files this rule applies to (e.g. src/**/*.py). Not needed for target: commit-msg rules.

exclude.description: Globs carved out of `paths` — vendored code, generated files, the check's own implementation.

check.description: Shell command that runs the check: reads the file list on stdin, exits 0 (pass) or non-zero (fail). E.g. becwright run forbid --pattern '...'

severity.description: blocking = stops the commit | warning = deterministic finding, does not block | advisory = best-effort / non-deterministic, reports but never blocks.

target.description: files = the changed files (default) | commit-msg = the commit message.
